factor_handling: automatic (glm conventions: sorted levels; treatment contrasts)
model_representation: single relation (coefficient rows + repeated model-level columns)
null_handling: Complete Case Analysis reported via n_obs/n_used/n_dropped; NULL predictors predict NULL
prediction_api: relation in -> relation out with <response>_predicted column
reproducibility_priority: R parity locked by pg_regress tests

sql_api: yes (procedure-style training functions + scalar predict functions)
formula_interface: no (independent variables as an SQL array expression string)
family_link: glm(): gaussian, binomial, poisson, gamma, inverse gaussian with link options; logregr_train(): binomial/logit
factor_handling: manual one-hot by the user (encode_categorical_variables exists as a separate preprocessing step)
model_representation: two side-effect tables: model table with parallel arrays (coef/std_err/z_stats/p_values; no term names) + <out>_summary
relation_in: partial (source table/view name as string; no inline subquery)
relation_out: no (fit returns void and writes tables by name; predict is a scalar function per row)
named_arguments: positional in documented usage (=> possible in principle)
null_handling: complete case at fit with num_rows_processed / num_missing_rows_skipped counters; NULL feature predicts NULL
metadata_representation: <out>_summary table stores call parameter strings (no structured factor levels)
prediction_api: scalar predict functions over the coef array + a hand-built feature array (manual join with the model table)
notes: Tier 1 (required experiment); verified with 1.21.0 on PostgreSQL 11 (docker/madlib); unseen factor levels silently scored as reference level
